I did that.

A role in the role of like a marketer or a marketing team.
And he ended up coming out with like the idea of workflows and stitching LLMs together and breaking down, having LLMs as a way to help people be more efficient, but still having people in the mix.

So he started trying to teach that to like to workshops and things like that.
And people are like, this makes sense, but it's too hard to do.
Can you do that for us?
So I ended up building an agency to do that.
And that's when I joined in August last year.
And we wanted to, like, it quickly became clear that not everybody can learn that.
And like, it's somewhat, it's this weird spot where like LLMs are like, oh yeah, 80% of the code is actually plain text.
But now you have this programming language that has no compiler that behaves completely different every time you change the text.
And you also have to switch them together in a distributed system where things crash.
And you have rate.
And
We ended up building this platform to orchestrate the LLM part, and we built another platform on top of that to help the people using these LLMs be more efficient.
we have two products today.
have the LLM orchestration or the AI workflow creation tool, and we have another one for content management.

In the future, they might, and we want to have a path to that, but we operate for them, and they get the outcome of our work.
And one of the big challenges we have today is that we have, like,

Understanding what the client wants and understanding what is possible is a big challenge and if you have just a marketer, they will come up with a bunch of ideas and pass that down to the engineering team, like some of the things we already have, some of the things we don't have, how can you know what is possible, what's not possible.

So we have this thing called like a strategy sprint now, where it's like up to eight weeks where people will just like learn everything from the client, come up with the foundational documents that will be used for LLM to do a good outcome, but also like understanding like do we need to create anything custom for this client, can this custom thing be reused for all the clients, how can we make this strategy sprint be more efficient, can we take more people if we optimize or automate some of the things there.

Like this strategy sprint is almost like a discovery that covers both human process and features that have to be built and workflows that have to be created and that's kind of the system you'll have today and this role would be at least.

For now, would be the person in that strategy sprint, almost being between clients, the marketing, the creator, marketing, higher level strategy folks, and then engineers, and the platform building, and the content production team.
And it's kind of a weird role.
It's not a traditional product manager.
Of course.
Yeah.
Yeah.

think, yeah.

just from a really, trying to organize my thoughts.

the team shifted, the engineering team, in their thinking and their focus.

lot of that was founder-led, but, you know, enablement, having the right.

it the open source?

The shift from mid-market to enterprise, massive.

I know you're going to work on connectors, which is a.

Enterprise is a platform play.

you.

And all the facets that go into that.
I think, like, I'm always curious about, because I think there's...

so often I'd have weeklies and we talk about like not necessarily what's on the roadmap or something like prioritized already, but just like observation.

I have my opinion on kind of prioritization.

Some things that matter to customers and there should be a good tie-in.
if there's anything that doesn't have clarity, like people kind of doubt why is this important, like that's my job to go and figure out.

And they can also like the thing that we got as the broad company writing guidelines, but not for their persona, which is like SMB for ads and that kind of community.
So we have to like, okay, like we have to create that, you know, and they can do it because they don't understand how to create this.
Like it can be, that has to be examples because LLMs do better with examples instead of telling me what to do.
So there's all those new ones around it and that LLMs are not ready to be a self-serving product.
Even like I said, like you understand it deeply, then you can create results.
If you don't, you just spit out inaccurate things, for example.
Yeah, I get it.
Anything else that I can share?
I'm actually good.
Yeah.
I'm good.
I'm good now.
I've enjoyed the conversation.
Thanks.
Yeah.
Same.
Just to share a little bit more of the process, it would be great if you could talk to Jason.
Jason is our head of growth, but he's also a former founder of a couple of companies.
He also runs a few accounts, a few clients as a way to dog food what we do and figure out the holes and all that.
So he's probably the most technical person that is not a programmer on the team and understands everything.
So, yes, I would just schedule something with you, Jason.
What's your time now?
You said you're interviewing with other companies and what is the process there?
I am moving slowly through things.
For me, I'm trying to just find, like, on that stability front, I'm trying to find a good fit more than anything.
And I've not been, I've been traveling the world for a while and just moved.
So I'm still early in the job search, to be honest.
And being pretty deliberate about who I'm talking to.
So, you know, I'll move as fast as you want to, but I'm also not like, you know, urgently trying to get something signed or anything like that.
So, yeah, happy to chat with Jason whenever he has time, but it doesn't have to be tomorrow.
Yeah, because we have 4th of July, and then I, yeah, and then next week I'm offered for Monday and Tuesday.
So, we're going to probably move a little slower than I usually want.
I'll try to get to talk to Jason tomorrow, if he's available and you're available.
If not, then maybe like Monday or Tuesday, if I really want.
If possible, great.
But, yeah, and I know Tucker's out this weekend, too.
So, I mean, like, yeah, happy for them.
Okay, so maybe early next week, like Monday or Tuesday.
Totally fine by me.
Yeah, no worries.
Perfect.
All right.
Thank you, Natalie, for going so long.
That was great talking to you.
Yeah.
excited to take that.
All right.
See you.
Great to meet you.
Bye.

## Meeting Purpose

[Hiring manager interview with Natalie Kwong for Product Operations & Innovation Strategist role at growthx.ai](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Natalie has a strong background in analytics, ops, and product management, with experience in scaling startups and transitioning from services to SaaS](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=555.0)
  - [The role requires bridging client needs, LLM capabilities, and business strategy while shaping product roadmap and features](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=1084.0)
  - [growthx.ai is evolving from a service-based model to a more scalable platform, focusing on AI-powered content creation and marketing workflows](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=1379.0)

## Topics

### Natalie's Background and Experience

  - [Started in ops and analytics, built teams and ran internal analytics across products, growth, and go-to-market](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=555.0)
  - [Transitioned to product management at Airbyte, focusing on growth and optimization](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)
  - [Set up analytics systems, including telemetry for open-source products and cloud-based solutions](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=628.0)
  - [Managed data activation, creating automations and flows to support the product lifecycle](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=686.0)
  - [Experience with tools like Segment, BigQuery, DBT, and Metabase](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=672.0)

### growthx.ai's Business Model and Challenges

  - [Currently operates as an agency helping clients become AI-native content marketing teams](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=383.0)

  - [Transitioning from services to a more scalable SaaS model while maintaining high-touch support](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
  - [Challenges in understanding client needs, determining feasibility, and balancing resources](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Need for a role to bridge sales, marketing strategy, and engineering capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### Role Requirements and Expectations

  - [Act as a "glue" function between clients, marketing strategy, and engineering teams](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Ability to understand client needs, LLM capabilities, and business strategy](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=1724.0)
  - [Shape product roadmap and features for internal tools and client-facing platforms](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=1724.0)

### AI and LLM Integration in Product Development

  - [Exploring ways to use LLMs for increased efficiency in product management tasks](https://fathom.video/share/W2i6tUVJEWPLb4hnNhFbk9pUyRVrbVny?tab=summary&timestamp=3076.0)
